notes: The multi-stage build correctly separates build-time deps (build-essential, gcc) from runtime
COPY --from=python-builder /wheels /wheels in app-builder is redundant — app-builder inherits from python-builder (OPT-D01)
python3 -m compileall -b copies both .py and .pyc into runtime; PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 only prevents new creation — doubling of the code (OPT-D02)
usermod -aG sudo user — the production user has sudo access (SEC-D01)
cron daemon run inside the container — an anti-pattern for Docker (OPT-D05)
libgdal-dev (dev package) in runtime — the runtime libraries would suffice
HEALTHCHECK defined — correct
chmod 777 /tmp/ — overly permissive permissions (SEC-D04)
chmod 777 /docker-entrypoint-initdb.d/restore_database.sh — a world-executable script
A simple Dockerfile for the dev DB
Adds nano (a dev tool) — ok for dev
chmod 777 -R /cert/* — TLS keys world-readable (dev-only, cert/ is self-signed — intentional)
The code is not mounted in the Dockerfile (volume mount from compose) — the correct dev pattern
Unlike the prod Dockerfile it has neither PATH=/scripts nor APP compileall — dev uses the source .py
Switches to USER root for mkdir/chmod then back to USER nginx — the correct pattern
Pinned Alpine version — a deterministic build
nginx-unprivileged — a correct security practice
The entrypoint dynamically injects the password from secrets into redis.conf using sed — a safe pattern
Pinned version — a deterministic build
3× separate RUN apt-get update without apt-get clean — enlarges the image (OPT-D03)
CMD runs two processes without PID 1 / signal handling: python3 fedora-init.py & catalina.sh run (OPT-D04)
Copies amcr.xsd directly from webclient/xml_generator — tight coupling between images

issues: DCD-01: logstash is missing depends_on: elasticsearch — race condition at startup (see also T02)
DCD-02: celery_worker run with -l DEBUG in production — excessive logging
DCD-03: GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD=/run/secrets/grafana_admin_password — the env var contains a path, not a password
DCD-04: ELASTIC_PASSWORD=elastic_pass — contains the secret name, not its value
DCD-05: LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD=logstash_elastic_pass — the same problem as DCD-04
DCD-06: Elasticsearch (9200), Kibana (5601), Grafana (3000), Prometheus (9090) exposed without isolation
DCD-07: selenium service in the production compose — should be only in the test compose
Practically identical to docker-compose.yml — a lot of configuration duplication
GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD the same problem as DCD-03
elasticsearch additionally has xpack.security.enabled=true — inconsistent with the prod compose (DCD-08)
backup_file_db_v4.tar is hardcoded in the compose ARG — manual update on a new backup
redis: build: ./redis + image: redis:8.4.0 — a confusing pattern of tagging a custom image as an official one
The ELK stack is missing — developers do not see logging in Elasticsearch
DCD-09: memcached:latest — a floating tag, a non-deterministic build
DCD-10: ELK version 8.19.0 vs. prod 9.3.1 — a major version gap
DCD-11: fcrepo-postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD=localdbpass1 — a hardcoded password
redis-exporter v1.80.1 vs prod v1.81.0 — a minor inconsistency
grafana 12.3.1 vs prod 12.4.0 — a minor inconsistency
logstash is missing depends_on: elasticsearch (present in dev, absent in prod — the reverse situation)
DCD-12: celery_worker and celery_beat build Dockerfile-DEV — inconsistency with prod (Dockerfile)
DCD-13: celery_exporter on the prod-net network instead of git-reverse-proxy — inconsistent network configuration
Secrets as plain files — requires preparing the files before deployment

git_docker-compose.override.yml.notes: A minimalist override that overrides the secrets for the git deploy

docker-compose-proxy.yml.notes: A standalone proxy compose — the correct separation of the proxy layer

git_docker-compose-proxy.yml.notes: Proxy for the git-deploy — builds from the proxy/ context

ghcr.io/osgeo/gdal:ubuntu-small-3.12.2.notes: Ubuntu-based GDAL image for PostGIS/spatial support — a suitable choice for a geospatial application

nginxinc/nginx-unprivileged:1.27.3-alpin.notes: Alpine-based nginx without root — a secure and small image

redis:8.4.0.notes: An older version than in the git deploy

fcrepo/fcrepo:6.5.1-tomcat9.notes: Fedora Commons Repository — the digital archive AMČR

docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elastics.notes: 9.3.1 in prod vs 8.19.0 in dev — a major version gap

security_issues.description: usermod -aG sudo user — the production container has a user with sudo privileges. If the application is exploited, an attacker can escalate privileges.
security_issues.recommendation: Remove the sudo group from the production user. If sudo commands are necessary for specific operations, set them in /etc/sudoers with NOPASSWD only for the necessary commands.

security_issues.description: GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD set to the path /run/secrets/grafana_admin_password instead of the actual password. Grafana does not support automatic reading of Docker secrets this way — the admin password will be set to the literal path string.
security_issues.recommendation: Use GF_SECURITY_ADMIN_PASSWORD__FILE=/run/secrets/grafana_admin_password (Grafana supports the __FILE suffix for env vars)

security_issues.description: ELASTIC_PASSWORD=elastic_pass and LOGSTASH_INTERNAL_PASSWORD=logstash_elastic_pass — the env vars contain the name of the Docker secret, not its value. Elasticsearch and Logstash will have these fields set to the literal strings 'elastic_pass' and 'logstash_elastic_pass' respectively.
security_issues.recommendation: Use an entrypoint script that reads the secret from the file and sets the env var: export ELASTIC_PASSWORD=$(cat /run/secrets/elastic_pass)
Use chmod 1777 /tmp/ (sticky bit, the standard Unix permissions for /tmp)

security_issues.description: POSTGRES_PASSWORD=localdbpass1 — a hardcoded password for Fedora Postgres in dev
security_issues.recommendation: Use a Docker secret in the dev environment as well (a file-based secret is ok for dev)

optimization_opportunities.description: COPY --from=python-builder /wheels /wheels in app-builder is redundant — app-builder inherits from python-builder
optimization_opportunities.recommendation: Remove line 37 (the redundant COPY) in the app-builder stage

optimization_opportunities.description: python3 -m compileall -b creates .pyc next to .py; PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 prevents writing in runtime, but the .pyc files from compileall are copied — both in the image
optimization_opportunities.recommendation: If the goal is a faster startup, compile into a separate directory and copy only the .pyc

optimization_opportunities.description: 3× separate RUN apt-get update without apt-get clean — unnecessary cache layers
optimization_opportunities.recommendation: Merge into a single RUN: apt-get update && apt-get install -y python3-pip python3-requests && apt-get clean && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

optimization_opportunities.description: CMD runs two processes (init + Tomcat) without PID 1 process management — signals (SIGTERM) will not be correctly passed
optimization_opportunities.recommendation: Use tini as ENTRYPOINT or move the initialization into an entrypoint script with exec catalina.sh run

optimization_opportunities.description: cron daemon in the production container — an anti-pattern. The cron tasks should be replaced with Celery beat tasks.
optimization_opportunities.recommendation: Move the cron tasks into the Celery beat schedule

version_inconsistencies.note: Major version gap — API changes between ES 8.x and 9.x, the default xpack.security settings differ
